commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(recess): escape-the-school game sandbox (#99)

* feat(recess): game engine, playable prototype, manage UI, and sandbox

Escape-the-school game with maze navigation, demon teacher identification,
gym dodgeball battles, and floor progression. Includes:

- Engine: maze generator (recursive backtracker), game state machine,
  challenge system, level config with difficulty scaling
- Play: 2D top-down playable game with keyboard/touch controls
- Manage: level param tuning (floors, maze size, teachers, demon ratio,
  strikes, difficulty) with live maze preview
- Sandbox: isolated testing of encounters and dodgeball battles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(recess): phase 1 — fix softlock, encounter spam, gym feedback, challenge variety

- Fix dodgeball loss softlock: keep demonsFound on loss, retry at gym
- Fix teacher re-encounter spam: skip auto-trigger for challenged teachers,
  add interact action (Space/Enter/ACT button) for re-engaging deliberately
- Add transient message system: gym shows remaining demon count, accusations
  show result feedback, messages auto-clear after 2.5s
- Expand challenge bank to 24 questions across 3 difficulty tiers
  (obvious/moderate/subtle) with varied answer positions
- Scale challenge difficulty by floor depth (top=obvious, bottom=all tiers)
- Larger mobile d-pad with centered ACT button for interactions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(recess): phase 2 — dodgeball mini-game, fog of war, player animation

Dodgeball rewrite:
- Two-phase per demon: dodge incoming ball by lane, then throw with
  oscillating crosshair timing
- HP system (demons+1 hits), demon roster with defeat tracking
- Timing windows tighten on lower floors
- VS intro screen, hit/miss/result feedback phases

Fog of war:
- Track visited cells in engine state with visibility radius
- Unexplored cells are dark, visited cells dimmed, adjacent cells bright
- Teachers only visible when adjacent (no peeking ahead)
- Player glow ring in fog mode
- Disabled in manage/sandbox modes

Player animation:
- Smooth 120ms CSS transition on player position

commit.message:
Fix mobile keyboard hiding send button + fix broadcast message delivery

Mobile: replaced CSS inset-0 (which sets bottom:0, overriding JS height)
with explicit inline styles via visualViewport — sets top/left/width/height
with bottom:auto so the panel shrinks to the visible area when the iOS
keyboard opens. The send button now stays visible.

Broadcast: replaced the setOpen() hack (abusing state updater to read
open state inside callback) with a proper openRef. The hack could cause
spurious re-renders and interfere with the Supabase channel listener.
Added subscription status logging for debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Symphonie Pro mix clipping and sample-to-instrument mapping

Scale mix output by 1/numPairs in sym_render() to prevent clipping when
multiple channel pairs are active (was hard-clamping at ±1.0).

Fix sample-to-instrument mapping in parseSymphonieForPlayback — sample
chunks map 1:1 with ALL instruments (Silent/Kill types get EMPTY_SAMPLE
chunks), not just PCM-capable ones. The previous pcmIdx counter skipped
Silent/Kill instruments, shifting all subsequent sample assignments to
wrong instruments. Now uses direct instrument index matching OpenMPT's
Load_symmod.cpp behavior.

Also fixes type 0 (Normal) instruments being excluded from PCM — they
are valid one-shot samples.

commit.message:
Add MinIO file attachments for Open Brain (#182)

* Add MinIO file attachment plan, ADR, and Open Brain docs

Document Phase 2a: MinIO as S3-compatible backend for Supabase Storage,
enabling image/PDF attachments in Open Brain thoughts. Adds ADR 0011,
updates PLAN.md architecture and implementation steps, and adds file
attachment how-to section to Open Brain docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add MinIO file attachment support for Open Brain

Enable MinIO in Supabase Helm chart with Longhorn PVC for persistent
object storage. Add brain-attachments bucket via SQL migration. Extend
MCP server with attachment upload in capture_thought and new
get_attachment_url tool. Point ArgoCD at refine-open-brain branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix MinIO: correct persistence key and add fsGroup for Longhorn

persistence.storage was wrong — the Helm chart uses persistence.minio.
Also add podSecurityContext.fsGroup: 65532 for the Chainguard MinIO
image which runs as nonroot and needs group ownership on the Longhorn PVC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY to seal-mcp-secret script

Fetch the service key from supabase-credentials automatically (same as
the DB password). Update docs to reference the script instead of inline
commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Re-seal MCP secret with SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch open-brain-mcp image to beta tag for testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch open-brain-mcp to beta image tag in ArgoCD Application

The valuesObject in the Application template overrides values.yaml,
so the tag must be changed here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace get_attachment_url with get_attachment that returns file content

Instead of returning a signed URL (useless to Claude.ai), download the
file from MinIO and return it as base64 with MIME type so Claude can
display images and read PDFs directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update docs: rename get_attachment_url to get_attachment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Open Brain client options analysis document

Weighs up Claude.ai, Claude Code, Slack bot, and web UI as client
interfaces. Documents the file upload context window limitation and
recommends a hybrid approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add local stdio MCP server for Claude Code

A lightweight Python MCP server that runs locally and wraps the Supabase
REST and Storage APIs. File uploads/downloads go directly over HTTP —
no base64 through the context window.

Tools: capture_thought, search_thoughts, list_thoughts, thought_stats,
upload_attachment, download_attachment.

Includes .mcp.json.example for configuration (copy to .mcp.json and
set BRAIN_API_URL, BRAIN_API_KEY, BRAIN_SERVICE_KEY env vars).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CLI MCP server command to use uv run --project

The entry point script wasn't found. Use 'uv run --project open-brain-cli
python open-brain-cli/server.py' instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update client analysis with Claude Code findings

Claude Code works for text capture and search but file uploads are too
clumsy — pasted images aren't on disk, requiring manual save-first
workflow. Slack bot is the recommended primary capture interface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Promote MinIO/Storage troubleshooting and foot-guns to docs

Add MinIO fsGroup, bucket sync, and PVC sizing issues to troubleshooting.
Add ArgoCD valuesObject override, SQL migration, and MinIO persistence
key foot-guns to CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove attachment upload from public MCP capture_thought

File uploads should go directly to Supabase Storage API (local CLI,
future Slack bot), not through the MCP context window. capture_thought
is now text-only. get_attachment (download) is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add MCP prompt to guide metadata extraction for capture_thought

Registers a capture_guide prompt that tells Claude.ai how to structure
metadata (type, topics, people, action_items) when capturing thoughts.
Based on patterns from Nate Jones' Open Brain companion prompts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move metadata guidance from MCP prompt into capture_thought docstring

MCP prompts aren't surfaced by Claude.ai — it only reads tool
descriptions. Move the metadata schema (type, topics, people,
action_items) into the docstring where it will influence behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add setup script and docs for local Claude Code MCP server

setup-brain-cli installs open-brain-cli and configures ~/.claude/mcp.json
with credentials. Can be run via curl|bash or directly from the repo.
Updated open-brain.md with Claude Code setup instructions and corrected
the file attachments section to reflect text-only MCP capture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Sync documentation with Open Brain implementation

Update 8 docs files to reflect actual state: text-only capture,
get_attachment tool, local CLI MCP server, MinIO deployed, Claude.ai
as primary client, Slack bot deferred.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix local MCP setup: add build-system, use claude mcp add

The open-brain-cli entry point was silently skipped because
pyproject.toml lacked a build-system. Also fix the setup script to
resolve the repo from the script's own location (not $PWD) and use
`claude mcp add` instead of manually writing mcp.json.

commit.message:
Initial commit: MCP service orchestrator

Docker Compose-based deployment orchestrator for MCP (Model Context Protocol) services
with a React dashboard, dependency-aware deploy engine, and real-time SSE status updates.

- Monorepo with shared types, Fastify backend, and Vite+React frontend
- Service catalog with tiered deployment profiles and dependency graph
- Phase tracking to prevent status polling from overriding active operations
- ESLint flat config, Prettier, Vitest tests, GitHub Actions CI, husky pre-commit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-calculate duration from start and end dates

Duration field now automatically computes from the date pickers
(e.g. Jun 12 to Jun 14 = 3 DAYS). Field is set to read-only
since it is derived. Also recalculates on restore from saved data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-fix DB sequences on startup, fix CORS error handling, remove old tg.sendData override

- All services now auto-correct PostgreSQL sequences on startup (prevents UniqueViolationError after migration)
- CORS middleware catches exceptions and always returns CORS headers (fixes "failed to fetch" errors)
- Removed obsolete submitBuy tg.sendData override — now uses direct API call to deal-service
- Wrapped publish_notification in try-catch so Redis errors don't break API responses

commit.message:
Add Solana integration, governance, bounty service, sovereign AI, platform sync

- Implement Solana RPC client with treasury, governance, and bounty program support
- Add governance routes (proposals, voting, delegation) in amos-platform
- Build bounty generation service with quality scoring and token rewards
- Add points economy system with contribution multipliers in amos-core
- Implement platform sync client (heartbeat, config sync, activity reporting)
- Add custom model provider support for sovereign AI (OpenAI-compatible API)
- Extend model registry to load customer-provisioned Qwen models
- Add self-hosted deployment mode with license key validation
- Update billing to skip compute markup for customer-owned models
- Add sync API endpoints for harness-to-platform communication
- Fix AMOS_DISCOUNT_BPS constant (was 200, corrected to 2000 = 20%)
- Update whitepaper and token economy docs for consistency
- Update README with deployment modes, sovereign AI, sync API docs

280 tests passing (54 core + 194 harness + 28 platform + 4 doc-tests)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add RENT to NCAL link step

The NCAL link template was missing the RENT option, causing
NCALIB members to lose the reentrant attribute. When included
in the final linkedit, the load module was marked as
NOT REENTERABLE, causing U0001 ABENDs when loaded by HTTPD.

commit.message:
fix: progressive one-by-one reveal for detected objects instead of CSS stagger (#63)

Replace resultsJustLoaded + animation-delay approach (all DOM elements
created at once causing layout lag) with JS-driven progressive reveal:
visibleObjectCount increments via safeTimeout at 120ms intervals, each
card enters DOM individually with fadeInUp animation for smooth feel.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: show .. entry in archive VFS listings (zip, tar, 7z)

All archive providers (ZipVfsProvider, TarVfsProvider, SevenZipVfsProvider)
now prepend a ".." entry to ListDirectory results, allowing the user to
navigate out of the archive with Enter/Backspace.

Parent path logic:
- At archive root → local directory containing the archive file
- In a subdirectory → one level up within the same archive

Add daily trip reminders at 22:00 MSK

- Send reminders 7 days and 1 day before trip departure
- Uses python-telegram-bot JobQueue (APScheduler)
- Iterates all users data files to check upcoming trips
- Add storage.all_user_ids() helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: prevent duplicate pipeline transitions (#138)

* refactor: proxy all Google Calendar calls through edge function

Remove all client-side token management (localStorage, gcalToken state,
refreshGcalToken, auto-refresh intervals). The edge function now handles
everything: status checks, event sync, event creation, and disconnect.
Client only knows "connected" or "not connected" — no tokens ever touch
the browser.

New edge function actions: status, sync, add_event (plus existing
exchange and disconnect). Internal getFreshAccessToken() helper mints
a fresh access token from the stored refresh token on every call.

Also handles the case where Google doesn't return a refresh token on
re-authorization (returns explicit error instead of silently failing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: deduplicate pipeline board views by address

Each deal now only appears in its most advanced stage on the board view
(Closed > Pending > Offers). Goal counts remain unchanged — they still
track all historical events. Applied to both seller and buyer pipelines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply pipeline dedup to both list and board views

The previous dedup only applied to the board view, so refreshing the
page (which defaults to list view) still showed duplicates. Now both
views share the same dedup logic via a single IIFE that computes
filtered arrays for both seller and buyer pipelines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate pipeline transitions for same address

Guard handleOfferStatus and handlePendingStatus so a deal cannot be
moved to Pending or Closed if that address already exists in the
target stage. Shows a toast instead of silently creating duplicates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use API valor_stock for dashboard (24,395€ not 102,400€)

Root cause: dashboard recalculated stock value from ingredientStore
before inventarioCompleto loaded, using raw precio instead of
precio/cantidad_por_formato, inflating value ~4x.

Fix: sum valor_stock directly from /inventory/complete API response
(same source as Inventario tab). Verified via SQL: 24,395.72€.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(serve): threaded request handling for concurrent clients (#87)

* feat(serve): switch to ThreadingHTTPServer for concurrent requests

Replace HTTPServer with ThreadingHTTPServer so each request gets its
own daemon thread. A slow embedding on one request no longer blocks
all others — critical for production deployment with multiple clients.

- daemon_threads = True ensures clean shutdown
- Thread safety: _QuarryContext is immutable after init, LanceDB and
  ONNX Runtime handle concurrent reads, cached_property eagerly
  initialized before serving
- New concurrency test proves two slow requests overlap (< 2x single)

Closes quarry-fb7

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document threaded request handling in DESIGN.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: eagerly init db cached_property, add concurrency test margin

Address Copilot + Bugbot review:
- Eagerly initialize ctx.db before serving (cached_property is not
  thread-safe, so all shared state must resolve single-threaded)
- Relax concurrency test threshold from 2x to 1.5x delay to avoid
  flaky failures on loaded CI runners

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rigorous audit: 10 proven theorems, 4 approximate, 2 falsified, 5 open

Reproducible Python script (rigorous_audit.py) classifies every CK claim:
- PROVEN: T1-T10 (BHML uniqueness, TSML characterization, unimodular,
  TSML singular, Being det=-7^3, Pfaffian, char poly, 2x2 eigenvalue=7i,
  unimodularity breaks at n=8, closure properties)
- APPROXIMATE: eigenvalue ratios (0.086%, 1.0% off), quadratic coeff (~1%),
  73% PCA variance vs 73% HARMONY (0.01% off), T* near-misses (~2%)
- FALSIFIED: SO(5) conjecture (dim=100 not 10), exact eigenvalue identities
- OPEN: 73 structural connection, B/D/BC universality, 15083 meaning
- TRIVIAL: tracelessness, imaginary eigenvalues, Jacobi (any symmetric pair)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
